content: Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Date 2026-06-20. Quick brain-dump: Cairn beta just finished with 12 testers; top ask was offline maps (9/12 explicitly). DECISION already made for product/monetization: freemium, premium at 4.99 EUR/month. I need to lock scope, timeline, and success metrics — and record action items. Thoughts: build offline maps as the first paid feature, keep price 4.99 EUR/mo, aim to launch by end of Q3 2026. Questions I need to answer now: which offline scope (city vs country), cost/licensing (OSM vs Mapbox), implementation approach (MBTiles/vector vs raster), and conversion targets to justify the dev effort.
City-level first (smaller packs) or country-level first (bigger packs) — which do you want to prioritize?

Leaning city-first. Numbers from testing: 9/12 testers asked offline, but only 3 said they needed whole-country downloads. Practical numbers: a single city pack ~50 MB, a regional/country pack ~200–400 MB depending on resolution. Team capacity: two mobile devs (Claire and Hugo) and me for backend/ML work. Timeline: start 2026-06-21, alpha 2026-08-15, beta 2026-09-15, public 2026-09-30. Licensing: prefer OSM tiles to avoid Mapbox recurring per-tile costs — we'll host MBTiles on S3 + CDN. Monetization: freemium core, premium 4.99 EUR/month includes offline maps + advanced routing & GPX export. Revenue scenarios (5% conversion for examples): 1) 1,000 MAU -> 50 subs -> 50 * 4.99 = 249.50 EUR/mo (2,994 EUR/yr). 2) 10,000 MAU -> 500 subs -> 500 * 4.99 = 2,495 EUR/mo (29,940 EUR/yr). Those are conservative baselines. So city-first seems the fastest path and minimizes per-user download size and hosting cost.
Which conversion target do we design for: conservative 2%, target 5%, or aggressive 10%?

Target 5% conversion as our product goal (design and funnels for that), but be prepared for a conservative baseline of 2% in forecasts. Product decisions: 7-day trial for new premium signups, billing via App Store / Play at 4.99 EUR/mo, per-account billing (teams share accounts in logistics). Immediate plan: free users can use live maps and basic routing; premium unlocks offline packs and advanced routing. CAC and exact marketing TBD, but initial spend should be minimal — rely on in-app prompts and email onboarding to move free users to trial. With 1,000 MAU and 5% conv we get 50 paid users (~249.5 EUR/mo) — enough to validate before scaling.
Offline maps MVP: download-only (tiles + cache) or include offline routing/search at launch?

MVP = download-only offline maps in v1. Rationale: shipping tiles + playback/visualisation is much faster; offline routing/search adds heavy algorithm work and larger index files — defer to v2. Implementation notes: produce MBTiles per city on the backend (Sam builds the pipeline), store on S3 behind signed URLs, serve through CDN. Mobile (Claire/Hugo) implement download UI, background download, local MBTile access, and per-account license check to unlock. Security/privacy constraint: offline maps must not upload user tracks by default. Start dev 2026-06-21, alpha 2026-08-15, beta 2026-09-15, public 2026-09-30. Metrics to track: offline-pack downloads, conversion rate from download -> trial -> paid, DAU, churn. Action items: create Jira epics today, assign mobile tickets to Claire & Hugo, I’ll build the MBTile pipeline and S3/CDN config, product (Léa) to draft pricing copy and in-app messaging.
What's one absolute 'do not ship' constraint we should enforce?

Do not ship with any paid per-download tile provider or third-party SDK that charges per-user downloads — that creates variable costs and destroys marginal unit economics. Also: do not ship offline routing in v1, and do not collect or upload user GPS tracks without explicit opt-in. Keep price at 4.99 EUR/month at launch, no immediate discounts. I'll document tasks in Jira, set sprints, and schedule a release review two weeks before each milestone (alpha, beta, public).
What's the single success metric we measure at 3 months post-launch?

Single success metric = 5% conversion among users who download an offline pack within 90 days of public launch, plus a secondary numerical target of 500 paying users by 2026-12-31 if MAU scales. If we hit 5% conversion on offline-pack users within 3 months, we consider the offline premium validated and iterate on routing and wider-region packs.
